1.4.11-non-text-contrast.remarks: Search box border (#e5e8e8, 1.23:1) does not need 3:1 contrast because the control is identifiable via magnifying glass icon, placeholder text, and prominent positioning. Focus indicator uses primary (#126ce0) at 4.94:1.
Checkboxes use neutral-dark (#626971) border at 5.56:1. Selected state uses primary (#126ce0) bg at 4.94:1 with white tick at 4.94:1. Excluded state uses error (#ce3f00) bg at 4.84:1. All pass 3:1.
Number input fields use border-neutral-dark (#626971) at 5.56:1 vs white, passing 3:1. Focus indicator passes.
Rating stars are paired with visible text labels ('3 stars out of 5'). Stars are not required for understanding per 1.4.11 graphical objects clause since equivalent text is displayed alongside.
Page buttons contain visible text numbers. Selected state border uses primary (#126ce0) at 4.94:1. Unselected buttons are identified by text content, not border.

Active stars (#f6ce3c) at 1.52:1 and inactive stars (#e5e8e8) at 1.23:1 both fail 3:1 against white. Stars are graphical objects required for understanding — aria-label is not visible to sighted users. Customizable via CSS custom properties. See KIT-5808.
Segmented facet buttons contain text labels identifying them. Border-neutral is supplementary. Selected state uses primary at 4.94:1.
Dropdown button contains visible text label. Border is supplementary for identification per 1.4.11 boundaries section.
